meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Solving the No Portfolio Trap: Client Workflow Guide
content.seo_description: Break the no-portfolio cycle. Learn how to build a professional client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first SaaS project.
content.answer_block: To overcome the 'no portfolio' trap, stop building toy apps. Instead, implement a professional client workflow. Use Antigravity to scaffold production-grade architecture and Claude Code to automate your development loop. By shipping a real-world solution for a hypothetical client, you prove your ability to deliver commercial-grade software.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank repository, the silence of a missing portfolio echoing in your career prospects. Every tutorial you finish feels like a hollow victory, leaving you with code that works but lacks the soul of a real-world product. The industry doesn't care about your 'To-Do List' app; they care about how you handle complexity, client requirements, and deployment.

It’s time to stop coding in a vacuum. By shifting your focus from 'learning to code' to 'executing a client workflow,' you transform from a student into a builder. This case study bridges the gap between theory and execution, leveraging modern AI tooling to simulate the high-stakes environment of a professional developer.

steps.title: Define the Client Problem
steps.description: Don't build features; build solutions. Define a specific pain point—like a CRM for local businesses—to give your project a clear objective.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a project requirements document for a local bakery's order management system, focusing on customer data privacy, automated email notifications, and inventory tracking.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a project spec file based on the bakery CRM requirements'

steps.title: Architect the Database Schema
steps.description: A professional app is only as good as its data model. Design a scalable schema that accounts for relational integrity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a PostgreSQL schema for an order management system with tables for Users, Orders, Inventory, and Notifications, including indexes for performance.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create migration files for the defined schema and verify integrity'

steps.title: Scaffold the API Layer
steps.description: Use your architecture to build a robust API. This is where your code meets real-world client needs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a secure RESTful API endpoint for creating and updating orders, including input validation and error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement the order controller and add unit tests for each endpoint'

steps.title: Integrate External Services
steps.description: Real projects use third-party APIs. Connect your app to a service like Stripe or SendGrid to prove you can handle integrations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service module to integrate SendGrid for transactional email notifications when an order status changes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure the email service and mock the webhook handler for testing'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Code is useless if it lives on your local machine. Deploy your app to demonstrate that you understand CI/CD.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Dockerfile and a GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing and deployment to a cloud environment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize deployment configuration and verify the build pipeline'

pro_tips: Always write your tests before the implementation code; it mimics TDD practices used by senior engineers.
Document your architectural decisions in a 'Design Doc' folder—employers value your reasoning as much as your code.
Use environment variables for every sensitive piece of data; never hardcode secrets, even in a portfolio project.
Implement basic logging early so you can troubleshoot production issues like a pro.
Focus on one 'hero' feature that solves a complex problem rather than five simple ones that do nothing.
content.next_steps: Once your app is deployed, write a blog post detailing the 'why' behind your architecture choices. Link this directly to your GitHub repo to create a high-impact portfolio piece.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for permission to be a developer. Build something that matters, learn the workflow that companies actually use, and turn your 'no experience' into 'proven delivery.' Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
